name: Vendor Manager
description: Ensures the organization's suppliers and their performance are managed appropriately to support seamless provision of quality products and services.

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions vendors, contracts, licenses, suppliers, or renewals
vendor-contract-expiry — Checks for contracts approaching expiry every Monday at 08:00
vendor-license-compliance — Audits license compliance on the 1st of each month at 08:00

actions.name: manage-supplier-record
actions.description: Create, update, query, and manage supplier records and profiles

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation: create, update, query, onboard, offboard

outputs.name: supplierRecord
outputs.description: Supplier record with profile and status

actions.name: manage-contract
actions.description: Track contracts, terms, renewals, and compliance

outputs.name: contractRecord
outputs.description: Contract record with terms and renewal status

actions.name: generate-scorecard
actions.description: Generate supplier scorecards and KPI dashboards

actions.name: assess-supplier-risk
actions.description: Evaluate and track supplier risks with mitigation plans

outputs.name: riskAssessment
outputs.description: Supplier risk profile and mitigation plans

actions.name: send-vendor-notification
actions.description: Send contract renewal alerts, performance alerts, and escalations

actions.name: manage-vendor-documents
actions.description: Manage supplier documentation, certifications, and audit evidence

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: Supplier escalation for vendor-managed CI incidents; vendor response times feed SLA tracking
Vendor-managed changes require supplier coordination and scheduling
Vendor-related root causes require supplier investigation and engagement
Underpinning contracts aligned with SLAs; supplier performance feeds SLA compliance
Vendor-managed CIs linked to supplier contracts; asset warranty and support tracking
Vendor documentation and support procedures maintained in knowledge base
Supplier costs feed IT financial management and cost optimization

hitlControls.reason: Adding or removing suppliers requires human approval
Contract changes require legal and management review
Escalating critical supplier issues requires human judgment
Activating contingency plans for supplier risks requires human decision
Formal dispute outcomes require human negotiation and approval
Reclassifying supplier strategic importance requires management review
Sharing organizational data with suppliers requires human authorization

knowledgeSources.description: Supplier profiles, contacts, and relationship data
Contract terms, renewals, and compliance data
Purchase orders and financial transaction data
